description: Full /sigint:start flow from elicitation through orchestration to findings output
prompt: Research the AI code assistant market. Priorities: competitive landscape, market sizing, trend analysis. Audience: investors. Timeline: this month.

expectations.description: Research orchestrator is referenced as the coordination agent
Dimension analyst agents are referenced as workers
Blackboard is used for team coordination
Findings are produced from the research
State file is referenced for persistence
Deprecated market-researcher agent pattern is not used
Deprecated TodoWrite tool is not used
Deprecated Subcog pattern is not used
Deprecated subagent_type pattern is not used
Elicitation captures all user context (audience, timeline, priorities), orchestrator spawns 3 dimension analysts (competitive, sizing, trends), and findings are written to state.json

description: Research findings are synthesized into an investor-tailored report
prompt: Generate a report from the AI code assistant research for investor audience in markdown format

expectations.description: Report synthesizer agent is referenced
Executive Summary section is included in the report
Data source is blackboard or state.json
Investor audience is acknowledged in the report
Deprecated Subcog pattern is not used
Report synthesizer reads findings from state or blackboard, generates investor-tailored report with Executive Summary, competitive landscape, market sizing, and trend analysis sections

description: Deep dive augmentation into a single regulatory dimension
prompt: /sigint:augment regulatory landscape for the AI code assistant research

expectations.description: Dimension analyst agent is referenced for the deep dive
Regulatory dimension is targeted
Regulatory-review skill methodology is referenced
Regulatory findings blackboard key is used
Deprecated market-researcher pattern is not used
Single dimension-analyst is spawned for the regulatory dimension using regulatory-review skill methodology, findings stored under findings_regulatory blackboard key

description: Research findings are converted into structured GitHub issues
prompt: Create GitHub issues from the AI code assistant research findings

expectations.description: Issue architect agent is referenced
Acceptance criteria are included in issues
Sprint planning is referenced
Deprecated Subcog pattern is not used
Issue architect extracts actionable items from research findings, creates categorized issues with clear acceptance criteria and sprint assignments

description: New research session recalls prior research from Atlatl memory
prompt: Research developer productivity tools. I previously researched AI code assistants - use those findings as context.

expectations.description: Atlatl recall is invoked to find prior research
Research tag is used for memory search
Prior AI code assistant research is referenced
Atlatl recall successfully finds prior AI code assistant research and uses those findings to inform the new developer productivity tools research session

description: Resume session combines state.json file restoration with Atlatl memory enhancement
prompt: /sigint:resume AI code assistants

expectations.description: State file is referenced for session restoration
Atlatl recall is used to enhance restored session
Deprecated Subcog pattern is not used
Session is restored from state.json file state and enhanced with Atlatl memories for richer context, suggesting next actions based on combined data

description: Init command loads Atlatl context and identifies active sessions
prompt: /sigint:init --topic 'AI code assistants'

expectations.description: Atlatl recall is invoked during initialization
Semantic knowledge or research tag namespace is referenced
Atlatl is explicitly named as the memory source
Deprecated Subcog pattern is not used
Initialization loads Atlatl context for the topic, identifies any active research sessions, and suggests the next action (start new or resume existing)

description: Large document source triggers source-chunker before dimension analysis
prompt: /sigint:augment competitive --source https://example.com/50-page-industry-report.pdf

expectations.description: Source chunker is referenced for large document processing
Chunking strategy is referenced
Dimension analyst processes the chunked content
Source-chunker is activated for the large PDF document, splits it into processable chunks, and dimension-analyst extracts competitive findings that are merged with existing research

description: Small document source is processed directly without chunker overhead
prompt: /sigint:augment competitive --source https://example.com/short-blog-post.html

expectations.description: Dimension analyst processes the source directly
Source chunker is not required for small documents
Small document is processed directly by the dimension-analyst without invoking the source-chunker, avoiding unnecessary overhead for short content

description: Contradictory findings across dimensions are detected and logged
prompt: Research the EV battery market. Priorities: competitive landscape, market sizing, financial analysis.

expectations.description: Conflict detection is referenced in the orchestration flow
Conflicting dimensions are identified by name
Resolution rationale is provided

When competitive analysis finds concentrated market share (few major players controlling most of the market) but sizing estimates suggest high fragmentation, the conflict is detected with specific dimension names, logged with structured context, and resolved with explicit rationale

description: Stale research findings are identified and refreshed with current data
prompt: /sigint:update --area 'competitive landscape'

expectations.description: Staleness or freshness of data is referenced
Memory capture is invoked to persist updated findings
Deprecated Subcog pattern is not used
Stale findings are identified by comparing timestamps, fresh data is fetched, compared with existing findings, state.json is updated, and new findings are captured to Atlatl

description: Status command displays live team progress dashboard
prompt: /sigint:status

expectations.description: Team status tracking is referenced
Completion status indicators are shown
Dashboard shows which dimension-analysts are active, complete, or pending, along with overall research progress and any blocked dimensions

description: Report command without active research gives a graceful error
prompt: /sigint:report

expectations.description: Error message indicates no research or session or findings exist
Graceful error message is shown suggesting /sigint:start, not a crash or stack trace

description: Full start flow uses exclusively v0.4.0 patterns with zero deprecated references
prompt: /sigint:start quantum computing hardware market

expectations.description: Deprecated TodoWrite is not used
Deprecated Subcog is not used
Deprecated subagent_type is not used
Deprecated market-researcher delegation is not used
Research orchestrator is used as the coordination agent
Atlatl memory integration is present
Task or Agent orchestration tools are used
Entire start flow uses only v0.4.0 architecture: research-orchestrator for coordination, dimension-analyst for workers, blackboard for state, Atlatl for memory, and TaskCreate/Agent for orchestration

description: Complete research cycle (start, augment, report, issues) uses exclusively v0.4.0 patterns
prompt: Run complete research cycle: start with quantum computing, augment competitive, generate report, create issues

expectations.description: Deprecated TodoWrite is not used anywhere in the cycle
Deprecated Subcog is not used anywhere in the cycle
Deprecated subagent_type is not used anywhere in the cycle
Research orchestrator coordinates the start phase
Dimension analyst handles augmentation and initial research
Report synthesizer generates the report
Issue architect creates the GitHub issues
Blackboard is used for inter-agent coordination
Atlatl memory is integrated
Full research cycle completes through all four phases (start, augment, report, issues) using only v0.4.0 agents and patterns without any deprecated tool, agent, or memory references

description: End-to-end research with methodology plan, framework application, and coverage matrix
prompt: Research the AI-powered drug discovery market. Run competitive, sizing, and trends dimensions. Show methodology plans, research progress, and a methodology coverage matrix at the end.

expectations.description: Methodology plan is written per dimension
Plans are surfaced to user
Coverage matrix is included in final output
Deprecated TodoWrite is not used
Deprecated Subcog is not used
Research applies methodology frameworks per dimension and produces a coverage matrix showing which frameworks were applied

description: End-to-end research with three-valued logic scenario modeling in trends dimension
prompt: Research the metaverse real estate market. Run the trends dimension and ensure three-valued logic scenario modeling is applied, not just trend listings.

expectations.description: State diagram is included in output
INC/DEC/CONST trend logic is used
Scenario generation is present
Terminal scenario is identified
Trends dimension applies three-valued logic scenario modeling with stateDiagram, not just a list of trends

description: End-to-end research with Porter's 5 Forces and explicit ratings in competitive dimension
prompt: Research the low-code/no-code platform market. Run the competitive dimension and ensure Porter's 5 Forces is applied with explicit ratings, not just a competitor list.

expectations.description: Porter's 5 Forces framework is referenced
Explicit ratings are present
All 5 forces are addressed
Competitive dimension applies Porter's 5 Forces with explicit HIGH/MODERATE/LOW ratings, not just a competitor list

description: /sigint:start with --topic reads topics[slug] block from sigint.config.json v2.0
prompt: /sigint:start --topic ai-code-assistants

expectations.description: Output references config lookup using the topic slug
Output passes config to research-orchestrator
Output does NOT read sigint.local.md as config source

description: /sigint:start detects old .sigint.config.json v1.0 and emits migration hint
prompt: /sigint:start market sizing research (with .sigint.config.json version:1.0 in context)

expectations.description: Output warns that the config is using the old v1.0 format
Output continues to function (does not fail) despite old format

description: /sigint:start with no config file present uses hardcoded defaults silently
prompt: /sigint:start supply chain risk analysis

expectations.description: Output proceeds to research-orchestrator delegation without config errors
Output does NOT report a config error or missing file error
Output does NOT reference sigint.local.md as a config source

description: /sigint:start loads context_file (CONTEXT.md) when present in topics block
prompt: /sigint:start --topic ai-code-assistants (topics block has context_file: ai-code-assistants/CONTEXT.md)

expectations.description: Output references loading or reading the CONTEXT.md file
Output passes context to the research-orchestrator

description: /sigint:start gracefully warns when context_file is configured but file does not exist
prompt: /sigint:start --topic ai-code-assistants (topics block has context_file pointing to non-existent file)

expectations.description: Output warns that the context file was not found
Output continues to research-orchestrator delegation despite missing context file

description: /sigint:start with --topic not in topics block falls back to defaults block
prompt: /sigint:start --topic unknown-topic (topics block exists but has no entry for 'unknown-topic')

expectations.description: Output does not error or abort on missing topic slug
Research session proceeds normally using defaults

description: /sigint:issues resolves default_repo from topics[slug] in sigint.config.json
prompt: /sigint:issues (active session has topic slug matching a topics entry with default_repo set)

expectations.description: Output references the repository from config rather than prompting user
Output does NOT reference sigint.local.md as repo source

description: /sigint:issues resolves default_repo from defaults block when no per-topic override
prompt: /sigint:issues (active session topic has no per-topic default_repo, but defaults block has one)

expectations.description: Issue creation proceeds using the defaults block repo
Output does NOT reference sigint.local.md as the source

description: SessionStart hook text references sigint.config.json (not sigint.local.md)
prompt: What configuration files does sigint use?

expectations.description: Output references sigint.config.json as the configuration file
Output does NOT reference sigint.local.md as a config file

description: Config cascade: topic-specific value overrides defaults block value
prompt:
/sigint:start --topic ai-code-assistants

Assume sigint.config.json contains:
```json
{"version": "2.0", "defaults": {"maxDimensions": 5}, "topics": {"ai-code-assistants": {"maxDimensions": 3}}}
```

expectations.description: The topic-specific maxDimensions value (3) is used
The defaults value (5) is not used for maxDimensions

description: Config cascade: project sigint.config.json overrides global ~/.claude/sigint.config.json
prompt:
/sigint:start research topic

Assume project ./sigint.config.json contains:
```json
{"version": "2.0", "defaults": {"maxDimensions": 4}}
```
And global ~/.claude/sigint.config.json contains:
```json
{"version": "2.0", "defaults": {"maxDimensions": 7}}
```

expectations.description: Project config value (4) takes precedence over global (7)
Research session proceeds normally
